text.title: Quality-Attribute Trade-off Analysis Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the trade-off-quality-attributes methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Trade-off analysis MUST start from concrete QA scenarios (Source / Stimulus / Environment / Artefact / Response / Measure). Adjective-only trade-offs MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Without scenarios, conflicts are subjective; scenarios provide testable inputs.

rule.statement: Report MUST name at least 2 cross-attribute conflicts and for each MUST cite specific scenarios in conflict.
rule.rationale: Unnamed conflicts surface as ad-hoc decisions; named conflicts are auditable.

rule.statement: Each conflict MUST identify the architectural sensitivity point (component / decision) where small change moves multiple attributes. The point MUST be referenced by element id in the architecture diagram.
rule.rationale: Sensitivity points are the leverage points for tuning + the risk hotspots for review.

rule.statement: Each conflict MUST carry a quantified resolution rule (e.g. 'accept p95 up to 220ms when monthly cost rises > 15%'). Qualitative rules MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Quantified rules can be applied mechanically and tested in CI / monitoring.

rule.statement: Report MUST list both risks (unresolved trade-offs that may bite later) and non-risks (attribute pairs currently aligned).
rule.rationale: Non-risks document what NOT to worry about; risks list the watch-items + their controls.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (scenarios, SLOs, diagram, stakeholders), apply r1 → r5 and emit the report.
rule.rationale: Order encodes risk: scenarios first, conflicts second, sensitivity third, resolution fourth, risk/non-risk last.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) single-attribute decision, (b) prototype, (c) current report already covers it. Exit with note.
rule.rationale: ATAM-style ceremony in these contexts wastes review.
